folio.label: 184v
folio.l: Witú Iewes were , venquysshed mygútily
Vn to the Iewes , swicú an hate had he
That he bad , Greithe his Char , ful hastily
And swor and seide , ful dispitously
Vn to Ierusalem , he wolde eft sone
To wreke his Ire on hit , ful crewelly
But of his påpos , he was lette ful sone
¢ God for his manace , hi~ so sore smote
Witú invisible wounde , ay incurable
That in hise guttes , karf it so & bote
That hise peynes , weren inportable
And certeynly the wrecche , was vnresounable
For manye a ma~nes guttes , did he peyne
But from his purpos cursed , and dampnable
For al his smert , he wolde hi~ nat restreyne
¢ But bad a noon , apparailen his oost
And sodeynly , or he was of it war
God daunted al his péde , and al his boost
For he sore fil , out of his Char
That it hise lymes , & his skyn to tar
So æ he neither , mygút go ne ryde
But in a Char , men a boute hi~ bar
Al forbrused , bothe bak and syde
¢ The wreche of god , hi~ smote so cruelly
That thurgú his body , wykked wormes crepte
And ther witú al , he stank so horribly
That noone of alle hise meyne , æ hi~ kepte
Wheither so he woke , or elles slepte
Ne mygút nougút the stynk , of hi~ , endure
In this myschief , he wayled and eke wepte
And knew god , lord , of euäy creature
¢ To al his oost , & to hi~ self also
Ful wlatsom was the stynk , of his careyne
No man ne mygút hi~ bere , to ne froo
And in this stynk , & this orrible peyne
He starf ful wrecchedly , in a Mounteigne
Thus hatú this robbour , & this homycide
That many a man , made , to wepe & pleyne
Swicú gerdoun~ , as bilongetú vn to pride
¢ The storie of Alexandre , is so comune
That euäy wygút , æ hatú discreciou~
Hatú herd som what , or al , of his fortune
This wyde world , as in conclusiou~
He wan by strengthe , or for his heigú renoun~
They were glad , for pees , vn to hi~ sende
